summary: The IDF conducted a precise strike on Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh, Beirut, killing three and wounding 15. Hezbollah launched a drone intercepted over northern Israel, while artillery and drone attacks continued near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin despite a ceasefire. Lebanese residents began returning to homes as roadblocks were imposed in southern Lebanon.
key_points: The IDF targeted Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh, Beirut, resulting in three deaths and 15 injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58592).
A Hezbollah drone launched from Lebanon was intercepted over northern Israel before midnight (Source: @manniefabian/58601).
Israeli forces continued artillery attacks near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin, including a drone strike on a vehicle, despite a ceasefire (Source: @presstv/194131).
Hostile aircraft intrusion was reported near Hanita and Idmit Tzofar, triggering a red alert (Source: @tzevaadom_en/68482).
Roads in southern Lebanon, including routes to Nabatieh al-Fawqa, Kfar Tabbani, and Marjeyoun, were closed by the Lebanese army (Source: @redlinkleb/25892).
Residents of southern Lebanon began returning to their villages following a tentative peace agreement (Source: @yediotnews25/131408).
Multiple false alarms of aerial threats were later identified as misidentifications in southern Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24340).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF struck Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh, Beirut, killing three and wounding 15. Hezbollah responded with a drone that was intercepted over northern Israel, while artillery and drone attacks persisted near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin despite a ceasefire. Lebanese road closures and civilian returns highlight shifting dynamics on the Lebanon front.

points: Iranian officials discussed a U.S.-Iran agreement that would permanently bar Iran from nuclear weapons, but no operational details were provided (Source: @asafroz/50336).
Iran and Oman announced plans to regulate maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3972).
Iranian deputy foreign minister stated that nuclear negotiations would only start after frozen assets are released (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3973).
Iranian officials warned of potential retaliation if U.S. commitments are not fulfilled, citing lack of resolve (Source: @kianmeli1/87985).
The IDF reported a precise strike on Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh, Beirut, causing three fatalities and 15 injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58592).
A Hezbollah drone launched from Lebanon was intercepted over northern Israel before midnight, with no sirens sounded (Source: @manniefabian/58601).
Hostile aircraft intrusion was detected at 15:06 near Hanita and Idmit Tzofar, prompting a red alert (Source: @tzevaadom_en/68482).
Israeli forces continued artillery and drone attacks near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin despite a ceasefire, including a vehicle strike in Kfar Tannin (Source: @presstv/194131).
Multiple alerts of aerial threats in southern Israel were later deemed false alarms (Source: @idf_telegram/24340).
Lebanese army closed roads to Nabatieh al-Fawqa, Kfar Tabbani, and routes from Kfar Remen to Sahl al-Maydana, affecting movement in the south (Source: @redlinkleb/25892).
Residents of southern Lebanon began returning to their villages amid a possible peace agreement (Source: @yediotnews25/131408).
Hezbollah drone activity was noted, but the drone was intercepted by Israeli forces (Source: @manniefabian/58601).
Lebanese National News Agency reported three deaths and 15 wounded from the Israeli strike in Beirut (Source: @manniefabian/58592).

key_developments: IDF precise strike on Hezbollah infrastructure in Dahieh, Beirut, killing three and wounding 15 (Source: @manniefabian/58592).
Hezbollah drone launched from Lebanon intercepted over northern Israel before midnight (Source: @manniefabian/58601).
Artillery and drone attacks continued near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin despite ceasefire (Source: @presstv/194131).
Hostile aircraft intrusion near Hanita and Idmit Tzofar triggered red alert (Source: @tzevaadom_en/68482).
Lebanese army imposed road closures on key southern routes, affecting civilian movement (Source: @redlinkleb/25892).
Residents of southern Lebanon started returning home following tentative peace talks (Source: @yediotnews25/131408).
Multiple false aerial threat alerts were later identified as misidentifications in southern Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24340).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah retains the capability to launch drones from Lebanese territory, as evidenced by the intercepted drone over northern Israel, indicating a persistent aerial threat. Israeli artillery and drone operations near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin suggest continued low‑intensity engagements despite a ceasefire, maintaining pressure on Hezbollah positions. Road closures in southern Lebanon reflect heightened security concerns and potential for rapid escalation if hostilities resume.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese authorities have restricted movement on several southern routes, aiming to control civilian exposure to conflict zones while monitoring Hezbollah activities. Israeli forces continue to enforce red alerts and conduct targeted strikes, signaling a readiness to respond to any escalation. International observers note the tentative civilian returns in southern Lebanon as a tentative de‑escalation signal, though underlying tensions remain high.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah drone launch sites in southern Lebanon is critical, given the recent interception over northern Israel. Continued surveillance of artillery positions near Nabatieh and Kfar Tannin is required to assess compliance with ceasefire terms. The pattern of false aerial alerts in Israel suggests possible electronic warfare or misinformation tactics that need further analysis.
